project_title: Determining the Preconsolidation Stresses of Low Void Ratio, Highly Overconsolidated Clays
abstract:
Determining the yield stress of low void ratio, heavily overconsolidated clays
is fundamental to accurate engineering design. Recent work has shown that
these materials often exhibit both a conventional preconsolidation stress (σ′p)
and an apparent yield (σ′ya) at a much lower stress. Incremental consolidation
(ICON) tests on Cumberland and Humphreys clays revealed two yield stresses,
highlighting the challenges of interpreting stress-strain behavior for soils with
complex consolidation histories. Using this data, this study investigates and
compares the available methods for estimating yield stresses, including
Casagrande’s graphical method, Janbu’s constrained modulus approach, and
Becker et al.’s work method to determine yield stresses. All of these methods
were originally proposed for test results exhibiting a single yield stress.
Comparative analysis of these methods underscores their effectiveness and
limitations in estimating multiple yield stresses, σ′p and σ′ya. Results provide
insights into the behavior of overconsolidated clays and guidance for selecting
suitable techniques for advanced geotechnical design.
